articles.title: Oral-B iO9 electric toothbrush review: Oral hygiene reimagined
articles.description: Oral-B's iO Series 9 electric toothbrush brings data-driven insights to your daily routine. With seven cleaning modes, a helpful pressure sensor, and real-time mouth zone tracking via a smartphone app, it aims to refine brushing habits. While not perfect, its advanced features offer unparalleled feedback for those seeking to optimize oral hygiene, though at a premium price.

Samsung Galaxy A37 The software update for both smartphones is currently seeding only in Europe, but the rollout should expand to other regions soon. articles.title: Zebronics Zeb Juke Bar 9710C review: Minimal size, maximum sound
articles.description: The Zebronics Zeb Juke Bar 9710C offers a compact 5.1 surround sound experience with wireless subwoofer and rear satellites, boasting Dolby Atmos and DTS:X support at an affordable price. It delivers solid bass and clear dialogue, making it a practical choice for those seeking immersive audio without complex wiring, despite minor remote usability quirks.

articles.title: Microsoft rejected her, Meta rejected her but Google said YES: Techie shares career lessons she learnt after months of unemployment
articles.description: A Gurugram-based software engineer, Priyanka Giri, has gone viral after sharing how she landed a job at Google following four months of unemployment and rejections from Microsoft and Meta. In a candid video, she pushed back against the belief that getting into top tech firms is nearly impossible, urging jobseekers not to “self-reject” before even trying. While acknowledging that luck plays a role, she stressed that consistent effort and applying without hesitation are key.

articles.title: Bernie Sanders quotes world's biggest AI scientists to warn everyone on AI; says: We must make sure that AI...
articles.description: US Senator Bernie Sanders is sounding the alarm on artificial intelligence, citing top AI scientists—including Nobel Prize winner Geoffrey Hinton and Yoshua Bengio—who warn the technology could turn against humanity. Sanders says Congress has done nothing since 1,000 experts, including Elon Musk, signed a 2023 warning letter. He's now hosting US-China AI scientists at the Capitol and pushing a bill to pause new AI datacenter construction.

articles.title: AI adoption to influence salary growth within 2-3 years: TeamLease CEO Shantanu Rooj
articles.description: AI is transforming Indian workplaces. Employees leveraging AI skills will see career advantages in the coming years. Sectors like technology, GCCs, and BFSI will lead this shift. Companies are integrating AI into core strategies, not just as tools. This will impact appraisals and career growth, especially in digitally intensive roles. Upskilling is now crucial for career advancement.

articles.title: Upskilling critical as AI disrupts India’s IT sector: Arundhati Bhattacharya, Salesforce
articles.description: India's IT sector faces a critical juncture as AI reshapes the industry, necessitating rapid workforce upskilling. Salesforce India CEO Arundhati Bhattacharya highlighted that while job disruption is unavoidable, new roles will emerge. She emphasized India's young, adaptable talent pool as a key advantage for continued global relevance, urging a proactive embrace of these transformative changes.

articles.title: Have machine in the loop: It is time to flip the human-in-the-loop model
articles.description: Two stories highlight how AI can empower rather than replace humans. In one, a software engineer used tools like ChatGPT and AlphaFold, along with genomics and mRNA science, to create a personalised cancer treatment for his dog, leading to significant recovery. In another, a visually impaired student built an AI-powered app to help people like him navigate daily tasks independently, using technology to “see” the world through sound.

They hope for lower spectrum prices to ensure cost-effective business purposes.

articles.title: Google Photos AI Enhance is now live for all users
articles.description: Google seems to be working hard on expanding its editing options in Google Photos. We recently saw the online titan add a quick facial touch-up feature. Now another similar feature has gone live. It is a very simple and straightforward “AI Enhance” button that sits beside the Enhance and Dynamic buttons that were already present in the Auto tab in the image editor. We tried it for ourselves, and while it can restore highlights and shadows quite effectively and deal with common issues caused by bad lighting and boost colors slightly, it is nothing truly spectacular in any way....
